event_headline: Fake Tacloban school shooting photos of Pangilinan, Hontiveros circulate online
topic: Integrity of Information and Social Media Manipulation

chronology.action: published an article stating that the Tacloban school shooting revived fears of online extremism
published a fact-check report confirming that photos of Kiko Pangilinan and Risa Hontiveros with Tacloban school shooting suspects are fake
warned that unrestricted access to cellphones and online content can expose minors to harmful influences and expressed support for age limits on social media

key_actors.name: Kiko Pangilinan
key_actors.role: political figure, subject of fake photos

key_actors.name: Risa Hontiveros
key_actors.role: political figure, subject of fake photos

key_actors.name: Rappler
key_actors.stated_position: highlights fears of online extremism and fact-checks false information

key_actors.name: Alfred Romualdez
key_actors.stated_position: warned about harmful online content for minors and backed age limits for social media

factual_claims.claim: Photos depicting Kiko Pangilinan and Risa Hontiveros with individuals identified as Tacloban school shooting suspects circulated online.
Rappler fact-checked these photos, determining them to be fake.
The Tacloban school shooting has revived fears of online extremism.
Tacloban City Mayor Alfred Romualdez warned that unrestricted access to cellphones and online content can expose minors to harmful influences.
Tacloban City Mayor Alfred Romualdez expressed support for age limits on social media.

disputed_claims.claim: Photos of Kiko Pangilinan and Risa Hontiveros with Tacloban school shooting suspects are authentic.
disputed_claims.competing_accounts: Rappler's fact-check concluded the photos are fake.

competing_frames.summary: Fake photos are circulated online to falsely associate political figures with criminal acts.

competing_frames.frame: Online extremism and harmful content risk
competing_frames.proponents: Rappler, Tacloban City Mayor Alfred Romualdez
competing_frames.summary: The circulation of fake content and unrestricted online access poses risks, particularly for minors and in fostering extremism.
Unrestricted access to online content exposes minors to harmful influences, necessitating measures like age limits for social media.
